name: Dyess AFB
profile: Military-friendly Dyess AFB housing snapshot anchored to Abilene, Wylie, Tye, and Taylor County, built for PCSUnited base-level market overviews.

market_metrics.market_type_summary: Affordable West Texas military market with fast-moving well-priced inventory and strong payment accessibility

mortgage_assumptions.defaults_note: Based on March 2026 Texas VA loan planning assumptions.

notes: Texas property taxes materially affect the true monthly ownership cost even when purchase prices look affordable.
Insurance, wind/hail exposure, utilities, foundation/soil conditions, and older-home maintenance should be included in all-in cost comparisons.
Dyess and Abilene can be highly affordable for disciplined VA buyers, but the best decision still depends on debt, assignment length, school needs, and property condition.

avg_home_mortgage_monthly.notes: VA loan planning estimate for practical Dyess AFB / Abilene-area housing, including Texas tax and insurance assumptions.

crime_status.installation: Extremely safe; gated military installation with controlled access.
crime_status.metro_abilene_taylor_county: Abilene varies by neighborhood. Military families should evaluate safety, school zones, commute, property condition, and resale at the address level, especially when comparing Dyess-adjacent pockets, Wylie, Tye, and central Abilene.

school_quality.on_base: No full on-base K-12 school system should be assumed; families should verify current school options and attendance boundaries by exact housing address.
school_quality.off_base: Wylie ISD and selected Abilene/Taylor County school zones are commonly evaluated by military families; exact assignment should be verified by property address.

rental_vacancy.notes: Rental inventory is influenced by military turnover, Abilene student demand, local employment, and family-size single-family availability near practical commute corridors.

rental_metrics.notes: Planning estimate based on Abilene-area 3-bedroom single-family rental observations and military-family affordability patterns.

military_lifestyle_fit.description: Strong bomber and airlift identity, tight-knit West Texas military community, low commute pressure, and affordable family housing compared with most military metros.

financial_brief.affordability_summary: Dyess is one of the more affordable Air Force markets for purchase price, but Texas taxes, insurance, utilities, and property condition must still be modeled before recommending buy.
financial_brief.buyer_opportunity: Strongest for disciplined VA buyers comparing Wylie, Dyess-adjacent Abilene pockets, Tye, and south/southwest Abilene by school zones, commute, and property condition.

market_bluf.bluf_summary: Dyess gives military families strong payment accessibility, low commute pressure, and practical VA-buying potential, but the best move depends on school zones, Texas tax drag, property condition, and assignment length.

summary_points: Dyess AFB sits inside an affordable Abilene housing market with low commute pressure and strong practical ownership potential.
Broader Abilene prices are higher than the immediate Dyess submarket, so users should compare specific neighborhoods rather than relying on one average.
Texas property taxes and insurance still materially shape the real monthly ownership cost.
Wylie, south/southwest Abilene, Tye, and practical Dyess-adjacent pockets can each fit different military-family profiles.
The best Dyess housing decision balances BAH, school zones, property condition, commute, debt, and expected assignment length.
opportunities: Lower purchase prices than many U.S. military markets.
Low commute stress can improve quality of life.
Stable military presence supports long-term housing relevance.
VA buyers can often find practical ownership paths when debt and target price are controlled.
Wylie and selected Abilene corridors may offer strong family fit and resale logic.
risks: Texas property tax drag can make monthly cost higher than purchase price suggests.
Older homes may need roof, HVAC, plumbing, foundation, electrical, or insulation review.
Neighborhood safety and school quality vary by exact address.
A fast days-to-pending market can pressure buyers to move quickly on good properties.
Short assignments require careful transaction-cost and resale-risk analysis.
buyer_notes: Dyess buyers should compare Wylie, Dyess-adjacent Abilene pockets, south/southwest Abilene, and Tye by commute, schools, condition, and all-in payment.
Do not assume low purchase price automatically means low monthly cost because Texas property taxes matter.
Property condition is critical in older Abilene inventory.
Wylie may command stronger family interest but can cost more than some Abilene pockets.
This market rewards buyers who preserve residual income and choose clean, resale-friendly properties.
seller_notes: Sellers should position homes around commute, VA readiness, condition, school-zone appeal, and practical monthly value.
Move-in-ready homes near Dyess or strong school corridors can appeal to military families.
Military buyers respond well to clear roof/HVAC/foundation information, clean inspections, and PCS-friendly timing.
Homes in strong family corridors should emphasize commute logic and low-maintenance ownership.
investor_angles: Military demand and Abilene’s local employment base can support rental relevance near Dyess.
Cash-flow math may be stronger than in higher-cost military metros.
Investors should stress-test property taxes, insurance, maintenance, vacancy, and tenant turnover.
Clean single-family homes with practical commute and strong condition may outperform cheaper but weaker-condition inventory.
buyer_guidance: Use all-in monthly cost as the primary decision filter.
Compare Wylie, south/southwest Abilene, Tye, and Dyess-adjacent pockets by commute, school zone, safety, property condition, and resale appeal.
Verify property tax, insurance, roof, HVAC, foundation, drainage, and utility expectations before finalizing a purchase budget.
Avoid choosing solely by lowest price or shortest distance to base.
Use PCSUnited calculators to compare rent, buy, residual income, commute risk, and assignment-length exposure.
seller_guidance: Price against current buyer payment sensitivity and active Abilene inventory.
Highlight commute logic, VA readiness, school-zone appeal, updated systems, and low-maintenance condition.
Homes with updated roof, HVAC, foundation confidence, windows, and clean inspection history should be positioned clearly.
Military buyers need practical value and confidence in monthly cost, not generic listing language.
landlord_notes: Military renter demand can support stable occupancy near Dyess AFB.
Single-family rentals in good school and commute corridors may appeal to military families avoiding purchase risk.
Investors should model taxes, insurance, maintenance, vacancy, and turnover conservatively.
Clean, functional homes near base, services, and schools may outperform cheaper but weaker-condition inventory.

market.market_type_summary: Affordable Abilene military market with strong payment accessibility and fast-moving well-priced homes

base_profile.installation_type: Bomber / Airlift / Global Strike and Mobility Support Base
base_profile.host_or_major_command: 7th Bomb Wing / Air Force Global Strike Command
base_profile.primary_mission_summary: Dyess AFB is a major Air Force installation in Abilene supporting B-1B bomber operations, C-130J airlift operations, global strike readiness, mobility support, and expeditionary mission capability.
base_profile.pcs_personality: Affordable, West Texas, family-relevant, mission-heavy, commute-light, school-zone-conscious, and strongly tied to Wylie, Abilene, Tye, and Taylor County housing choices.
base_profile.base_bluf: Dyess is one of the more financially accessible Air Force markets, but it is still a tax, insurance, school-zone, property-condition, and resale decision. Low commute stress is a strength, but the best housing move depends on exact neighborhood and all-in monthly cost.

user_positioning.military_family_bluf: Prioritize school-zone verification, commute simplicity, property condition, BAH fit, taxes, insurance, and assignment length before signing a lease or purchase contract.
user_positioning.single_airman_bluf: Prioritize short commute, rent control, roommate flexibility, access to daily services, and avoiding unnecessary overbuying if the assignment timeline is short.
user_positioning.realtor_bluf: The strongest Dyess housing conversations are about VA-ready condition, Wylie versus Abilene tradeoffs, tax-aware payment math, school-zone confidence, and commute simplicity.
user_positioning.investor_bluf: Rental demand near Dyess is influenced by military turnover, Abilene employment, university demand, and the need for clean single-family homes in practical commute corridors.

base_map_image.alt: PCSUnited public-facing Dyess AFB placeholder orientation image for base housing and relocation planning.
base_map_image.title: Dyess AFB Base & Housing Overview
base_map_image.caption: Placeholder PCSUnited orientation image until a dedicated Dyess gate and neighborhood map is available.
base_map_image.safety_note: This is not an official DoD map. It is a placeholder PCSUnited orientation image and should not be used for access control, emergency routing, or official navigation.

visitor_control_center.name: Dyess AFB Visitor Control Center
visitor_control_center.address: 1518 East Arnold Boulevard, Dyess AFB, TX 79607
visitor_control_center.hours: Monday-Friday, 7:00 a.m. to 9:00 p.m.; closed Saturday, Sunday, holidays, and family days; verify current hours before travel
best_for: Family members without routine installation access
visitor_control_center.map_zone: Main Gate / Front Gate visitor processing area
visitor_control_center.note: Use official Dyess AFB visitor information before arrival. Access requirements, sponsor rules, gate procedures, and hours can change.

gates.name: Main Gate / East Gate
gates.hours: 24/7; verify official Dyess AFB gate hours before travel
gates.commute_notes: Primary 24-hour access point for many users approaching from Abilene and Arnold Boulevard corridors.
gates.user_warning: Verify current gate status with official Dyess AFB sources before travel.

gates.name: Tye Gate
gates.hours: Monday-Friday, 5:30 a.m. to 5:30 p.m.; closed holidays; verify current hours before travel
gates.commute_notes: Useful for users living in Tye or northwest Abilene when schedule and gate hours align.
gates.user_warning: Limited weekday hours matter. Do not use this as a default route without checking official access status.

gates.name: Commercial Gate
gates.hours: Verify official Dyess AFB commercial access procedures
gates.commute_notes: Useful for commercial or delivery planning, not a default family commute gate.
gates.user_warning: Movers and commercial deliveries should verify procedures through official base access guidance.

major_services.name: Dyess Medical Clinic
major_services.address: Verify current clinic address before travel
major_services.phone: Verify through official TRICARE / Dyess medical source
major_services.hours: Verify current clinic and pharmacy hours
major_services.user_note: Treat this as a major medical anchor for PCS planning. Verify emergency care options, pharmacy procedures, and clinic availability directly with official medical sources.

major_services.name: Dyess Commissary
major_services.address: Verify current commissary address before travel
major_services.phone: Verify current phone number with DeCA
major_services.user_note: Commissary access can materially affect monthly food budget for eligible users.

major_services.name: Dyess Exchange
major_services.address: Verify current Exchange location before travel
major_services.phone: Verify current phone number with AAFES
major_services.user_note: Important daily-life anchor for new arrivals, families, and permanent party.

major_services.name: Military Clothing Store
major_services.user_note: Useful for military-specific shopping needs after arrival.

major_services.name: Dyess AFB Military Housing Office
major_services.address: Verify current housing office address before travel
major_services.user_note: Military families should contact the housing office early for current local housing conditions, referral services, and relocation assistance.

major_services.name: Dyess Family Homes
major_services.address: Verify current leasing office address before travel
major_services.email: Verify current leasing contact through housing provider
major_services.user_note: On-base housing can simplify commute and reduce market uncertainty, but families should compare BAH capture, waitlists, pet policies, and off-base affordability.

major_services.address: Verify current community address before travel
major_services.phone: Verify current phone number with housing provider
major_services.email: Verify current leasing contact through housing provider
major_services.user_note: Quail Hollow is described as a family-friendly military housing community near Dyess and Abilene; users should verify current eligibility, pricing, BAH rules, and availability.

major_services.name: Unaccompanied Housing Office
major_services.address: Verify current office location before travel
major_services.phone: Verify current phone number with official installation sources
major_services.email: Verify current email with official installation sources

major_services.address: Verify current branch or ATM location before travel
major_services.user_note: Specific bank or credit union locations may change. Use current official branch locator or base directory before routing users.

major_services.address: Verify current CDC, youth center, and school liaison locations before travel
major_services.user_note: Critical for families. Exact program availability, waitlists, and eligibility should be verified with official Dyess AFB resources.

on_base_housing.housing_type: Privatized and military housing support environment
on_base_housing.housing_office_address: Verify current housing office address before travel
on_base_housing.privatized_housing_provider: Dyess Family Homes / Quail Hollow Family Housing
on_base_housing.map_zone: housing support area / family housing communities
best_for: Families wanting to reduce commute uncertainty
Users who want predictable access to base services
watchouts: Availability and waitlists can change
On-base housing may capture BAH differently than off-base renting
Pet policies and breed restrictions should be verified
School assignment must be verified by exact address
Off-base options in Wylie, Abilene, and Tye may be financially attractive and should be compared
Texas property taxes and insurance should be modeled for off-base purchase scenarios
on_base_housing.pcsu_strategy_note: Compare on-base housing against off-base rent and mortgage options. Around Dyess, the best choice depends on rank, dependents, BAH, debt, commute tolerance, school priorities, Texas tax exposure, property condition, pet needs, and expected assignment length.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Wylie
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Popular family-oriented corridor with strong school reputation, suburban feel, and practical commute to Dyess.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 15-30 minutes depending on exact address, gate, and traffic
likely_gate_logic: Other gates depending on duty location and current access rules
watchouts: Prices may be higher than older Abilene pockets
School assignment should be verified by exact address
Texas taxes and insurance should be included in payment planning
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong first-look area for families who want school confidence, resale appeal, and manageable commute.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: South Abilene
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Practical family and commuter option with broad inventory, services, and manageable access to Dyess.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 10-25 minutes depending on exact address and gate
likely_gate_logic: Tye Gate depending on route and current access rules
watchouts: Property age and condition vary
Some pockets command stronger pricing
School assignment and resale appeal should be verified
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong practical option for users who want short commute, services, and good affordability.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Southwest Abilene
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Strong practical corridor for families and buyers who want access to shopping, schools, and manageable commute.
best_for: Buyers prioritizing newer or better-maintained inventory
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 10-25 minutes depending on exact address and gate
likely_gate_logic: Tye Gate depending on route and current access rules
watchouts: Some areas may price higher than immediate Dyess-adjacent pockets
HOA or newer subdivision costs should be checked
Property tax and insurance assumptions should be reviewed
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Excellent balance candidate for buyers who want family fit, services, and payment discipline.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Dyess-adjacent Abilene
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Very short commute potential with strong affordability, but neighborhood and property-condition screening matter.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 5-15 minutes depending on exact address and gate
watchouts: Neighborhood quality varies
Older homes may require stronger inspection
School and resale fit should be reviewed carefully
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Good for commute-first users if the exact property condition and neighborhood fit are strong.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Tye
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Small-town option near Dyess with short commute potential, lower traffic, and practical affordability.
best_for: VA buyers comfortable with smaller-market inventory
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 10-20 minutes depending on exact address and gate availability
likely_gate_logic: Main Gate when Tye Gate is unavailable
watchouts: Tye Gate hours are limited and should be verified
Inventory may be limited
Some properties may have rural utility or maintenance considerations
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong commute and affordability candidate when the gate schedule, school needs, and property condition fit.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Buffalo Gap / Tuscola
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Quieter southern small-town options with space and family appeal, but longer commute and inventory tradeoffs.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 25-45+ minutes depending on exact address, route, and gate
likely_gate_logic: Other routes depending on duty location
watchouts: Longer commute than Abilene or Tye
Inventory may be limited
Rural utility, internet, and maintenance details should be verified
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Best for users intentionally seeking quieter living and more space with an accepted commute tradeoff.

commute_intelligence.overall_commute_posture: Very favorable compared with most military metros because Abilene traffic is manageable and practical housing corridors are close to Dyess.
primary_commute_risks: Choosing by lowest price instead of school, condition, and resale fit
Limited Tye Gate hours for some commute patterns
Longer drives from Buffalo Gap, Tuscola, or extended rural areas
Weather, wind, hail, and road conditions during severe weather
planning_guidance: Test the commute during the user’s expected report-time window.
Confirm whether Main Gate or Tye Gate best supports the user’s home location and duty schedule.
Compare Wylie, south/southwest Abilene, Tye, and Dyess-adjacent pockets by route reliability, schools, and property condition.
Verify tax and insurance assumptions before treating a property as affordable.
Use commute plus all-in housing cost as the core PCSUnited decision screen.
commute_intelligence.pcsu_bluf: Dyess commute risk is low, but the right housing choice still depends on school zones, property condition, Texas tax drag, and the user’s assignment timeline.

bah_market_reality.buying_power_summary: Dyess offers strong buying power for many military households because Abilene purchase prices are lower than most military metros, though Wylie and stronger corridors may cost more.
bah_market_reality.renting_summary: Renting remains practical for short assignments or users still learning Abilene, but ownership can be compelling when the property is clean, resale-friendly, and safely within monthly limits.
bah_market_reality.pcsu_strategy_note: Dyess should be modeled as a strong affordability market, but PCSUnited should still test rank, BAH, debt, savings, credit score, family size, commute tolerance, school needs, property condition, and assignment length before recommending buy versus rent.

strengths: Wylie and south/southwest Abilene family corridors
Practical access to services, universities, healthcare, and regional amenities
watchouts: Texas property taxes and insurance can raise monthly cost
School assignments must be verified by exact address
Older homes may need roof/HVAC/foundation review
Some cheaper properties may have weaker resale or condition concerns
Short assignments require careful rent-versus-buy analysis
family_readiness.pcsu_family_bluf: Dyess can be a strong family PCS location when the household chooses the right Abilene corridor, verifies schools, protects residual income, and avoids weak-condition properties.

arrival_checklist: Verify current gate hours and visitor access procedures through official Dyess AFB sources.
Contact the Dyess AFB Military Housing Office early for current local housing conditions and relocation support.
Confirm whether on-base housing or off-base housing better fits rank, dependents, BAH, pets, school needs, and expected assignment length.
Compare Wylie, south/southwest Abilene, Dyess-adjacent areas, Tye, Buffalo Gap, and Tuscola using commute, schools, safety, condition, and all-in monthly cost.
Check property tax, insurance, roof, HVAC, foundation, drainage, utilities, HOA, and wind/hail exposure before finalizing a purchase budget.
Verify school zones by exact property address.
Use PCSUnited calculators to compare rent, buy, residual income, commute risk, and assignment-length exposure before committing.
pcs_watchouts: Do not assume low purchase price means low all-in monthly cost because Texas taxes matter.
Do not choose by distance alone; school zone, condition, and resale matter.
Do not ignore older-home maintenance or foundation risk.
Verify schools, taxes, insurance, HOA, and utilities before calling a home affordable.
Short assignments may favor renting or on-base housing if resale risk is high.
A fast-moving local market can pressure buyers to waive discipline; keep the numbers in control.

realtor_intelligence.best_client_positioning: Lead with tax-aware payment math, VA-ready condition, school-zone confidence, commute simplicity, and assignment-length strategy. Dyess clients need practical West Texas housing guidance.
strong_listing_angles: Wylie or strong Abilene school-zone positioning
buyer_consultation_angles: Rent versus buy based on assignment length
All-in payment including taxes, insurance, HOA, utilities, and maintenance
Wylie versus Abilene versus Tye tradeoffs
Property condition and VA appraisal readiness
Future resale appeal to military and Abilene buyers
investor_consultation_angles: University and local employment renter demand
realtor_intelligence.pcsu_realtor_bluf: A strong Dyess realtor should sound like a military housing strategist, VA-condition filter, and Texas-tax-aware affordability advisor — not just a local salesperson.

notes: Market and housing values are PCSUnited planning estimates and should be refreshed periodically.
Gate, visitor, service, medical, and housing office details should be verified against official installation sources before publication or use in routing.
Placeholder map image is used until a dedicated Dyess base orientation map is created.
Dyess-area values differ from broader Abilene values, so PCSUnited should keep both immediate base-area and broader Abilene context in mind.

source_notes: Use official Dyess AFB and Military OneSource sources for gate hours, visitor control, newcomer guidance, and installation access.
Use official Air Force Housing, Dyess Military Housing Office, Dyess Family Homes, and Quail Hollow sources for housing details.
Use DeCA, AAFES, and TRICARE sources for commissary, exchange, and medical details.
Use Zillow-style indexes, Realtor.com-style market data, local MLS, rental aggregators, HUD, and Census-style demographic sources for housing and market refreshes.
base_profile.disclaimer: PCSUnited base profiles are planning tools only. They are not official DoD, Air Force, installation, legal, financial, real estate, lending, insurance, school-zone, or navigation guidance. Users should verify gate access, housing rules, school zones, medical services, visitor procedures, market prices, insurance estimates, commute conditions, utility costs, and property condition through official and professional sources before making decisions.

demographics.provider: US Census Bureau / Abilene and Taylor County planning estimates

rent.provider: Rental listing aggregators / Abilene-area planning estimates

base_profile.provider: PCSUnited planning profile using public-facing installation, housing, and market research structure

parser_notes: Built in the PCSUnited city/base JSON pattern used by Lackland, Andrews, Barksdale, Beale, Cannon, Charleston, Davis-Monthan, and Dover.
All major PCSUnited fields included and adapted for Dyess AFB.
Compatible with PCS Snapshot, Base Demographics Shell, Base SaaS, and TOR parsing.
Preserves duplicate value aliases for avg_home_value, average_home_value, avgHome, and city_avg_home.
Preserves duplicate median price aliases for median_list_price, median_sold_price, and median_sale_price.
Preserves by_bedroom entries for 2, 3, 4, and 5-bedroom Market Insight compatibility.
